sprint: Magic Card Identity Evolution -- UI Polish Pass
task: A direct follow-up after the schema was run live and all four Magic Card screens were screenshotted: 'i think we can do better work on UI and UX on these screens.' Traced every complaint to a real, specific cause. The real bug: .magic-card-overlay centered content with plain align-items:center on a scrollable container -- when a panel is taller than the viewport, scrollTop starts at 0, showing the vertically-centered middle and permanently clipping the top, the exact 'stars cut off at the top of the screen' artifact in the report. Fixed with align-items:flex-start plus align-items:safe center (upgrades to true centering only when content fits, falls back to the safe baseline otherwise); .magic-card-home-panel/.magic-card-gate-panel additionally gained their own max-height+overflow-y:auto so a tall panel scrolls internally as a self-contained modal card. Verified directly via Playwright diagnostics under a deliberately short 460px viewport: the panel's own top element stays fully visible at scrollTop:0 even with scrollHeight nearly 2.3x clientHeight. A second real bug: clicking 'Not you?' on the single-card Identity Gate rebuilt the panel into a picker grid but never removed the Not You button itself, leaving it as an orphaned heading floating above the grid -- fixed in _renderPicker(), which now also removes the stale button and inserts a proper 'Whose adventure is this?' heading when none exists. Visual polish: the picker's two action tiles (Begin Exploring / Recall a different card) now use icon-circles matching a real card tile's visual footprint so all three read as equal peers; js/magicCardArt.js's drawFront() devoted roughly 70% of its own canvas to blank space, rebalanced with a deterministic starfield, a soft atmospheric glow, the nickname moved to vertical middle, and a quiet divider+tagline; Screen 1's recall/redeem widget (shared by both the Vihu Card and Magic Card widgets) gained a warm paper-toned card frame around the previously-unframed dark tap board, and the board itself widened from 360px to 420px. Verified end-to-end via a new Playwright suite with non-localhost network requests aborted so a live Supabase call fails fast instead of hanging. Full regression across both Phase 1 suites (23/23, 7/7) and the Phase 2 suite (21/21) passes unchanged, plus both goldenBuild.js suites pass unchanged.
